top_stocks.description:
• Alamos Gold Inc. is a precious-metals mining company focused on gold production (with operations and projects in Canada and Mexico).
• Market cap and size classification: mid-cap gold miner; exact market-cap can vary with price moves (confirm current market cap if needed).
• Key competitive advantage: geographic and asset diversification across multiple producing districts, plus ongoing mine/expansion guidance intended to support lower all-in sustaining costs (AISC) and longer-life production profiles.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Feb 2026: Alamos Gold reported Q4 and full-year 2025 results and highlighted expansion/production and cost guidance for 2026–2028, including an Island Gold District expansion framework.
• Feb 2026: Alamos Gold provided three-year operating guidance projecting 46% production growth by 2028 and significantly lower costs, including 2026 production guidance of 570,000–650,000 ounces and 2028 guidance of 755,000–835,000 ounces.
• 2026 (latest): The company also announced an Island Gold District Expansion Study (and related mill expansion items) with production and cost implications starting in the 2028 timeframe.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=23.54 and PEG=0.33 suggest the market price may not fully reflect growth prospects; PEG below 1 typically implies a more favorable growth-at-a-price relationship (verify against consensus growth and whether earnings are normalized vs one-offs).
• Profitability: ROE=0.2206% and Profit Margin=0.4897% look low, so the business may be showing thin accounting margins in the underlying metric set—important to reconcile whether this reflects cyclical earnings, non-cash items, or balance-sheet effects.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.4128% is modest; paired with the low PEG, the key question is whether growth is understated due to normalization (e.g., commodity-cycle) or because near-term EPS is temporarily constrained.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 indicates strong screening results versus your factor model; given the low ROE/profit margin readings, this selection is likely driven by valuation/growth/other quality signals beyond accounting margin alone.

top_stocks.description:
• Broadcom Inc. designs and supplies semiconductor solutions and infrastructure software, with a large presence in data-center, networking, and enterprise infrastructure.
• Market cap and size classification: mega-cap; a large, system-scale infrastructure vendor.
• Key competitive advantage: scale and breadth across chips and infrastructure software, with strong positioning in AI-related accelerators and AI networking as well as a mature enterprise software footprint.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Mar 4, 2026: Broadcom reported first quarter fiscal 2026 results (quarter ended Feb 1, 2026), with revenue of $19.31B (+29% YoY) and AI-related revenue of $8.4B (+106% YoY).
• Mar 2026 (same results cycle): Broadcom guided second quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of approximately $22B (vs prior-year comparison implying strong growth) alongside operating/investment signals tied to AI infrastructure demand.
• Mar 2026 (follow-on): Media coverage and commentary around the results emphasized “surging AI” demand and continued momentum in AI accelerators and AI networking tied to management’s outlook.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=79.3 with PEG=0.46 implies a premium multiple supported (at least in your model) by growth; PEG well below 1 can be seen as reasonable, but the high P/E means execution risk is meaningful if growth slows.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3337% and Profit Margin=0.3657% are relatively low as shown in your metric set; investors typically focus more on broad cash generation and segment-level profitability—so you should sanity-check whether these margin/ROE measures are distorted by one-time items, capital structure, or metric definitions.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.4864% is moderate; given the high P/E, the investment case depends on whether AI-driven earnings power can persist and whether those growth estimates are understated.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 indicates very strong composite factor alignment; paired with the strong AI revenue growth reported for the most recent quarter, the score appears consistent with a “fundamentals + momentum” profile.

top_stocks.description:
• NVIDIA Corporation designs GPUs and accelerated computing platforms used for AI training/inference and data-center workloads.
• Market cap and size classification: mega-cap; a leading provider of AI compute hardware and related systems/software.
• Key competitive advantage: software/hardware platform integration (CUDA ecosystem and full-stack approach) plus rapid platform roadmap execution and strong demand for AI infrastructure components.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Mar 16, 2026: NVIDIA announced BlueField-4 STX storage architecture at GTC 2026, positioning it for long-context/agentic AI workloads and accelerated storage infrastructure.
• 2026 (earnings/updates): NVIDIA’s most recent earnings cycle discussed strong data-center revenue and continued AI infrastructure momentum, with guidance/outlook updates included in company materials.
• Mar 2026 (market coverage): Industry reporting around GTC and the broader AI infrastructure theme highlighted ongoing platform expansions and system-level integration announcements.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=41.15 and PEG=0.46 indicate a growth-supported valuation; PEG below 1 can be interpreted as “not expensive relative to growth,” but the absolute P/E still embeds optimism.
• Profitability: ROIC=71.75% is extremely strong, and Profit Margin=0.556% supports the notion that the business earns attractive economic returns (confirm your margin metric definition because the numeric is unusually small versus typical GAAP/operating margin conventions).
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.3956% is modest in your model; NVIDIA’s historical growth is much higher, so you should validate whether your EPS growth inputs are conservative, based on normalized earnings, or limited by conservative consensus.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 plus very high ROIC suggests your model views NVIDIA as high quality; the main watch-outs would be export/regulatory constraints and whether AI demand sustains at the implied earnings trajectory.

top_stocks.description:
• Micron Technology, Inc. manufactures memory and storage semiconductor products, including DRAM, NAND flash, and related technologies used in AI/data-center and consumer systems.
• Market cap and size classification: large-cap semiconductor manufacturer; a major supplier in the memory cycle.
• Key competitive advantage: scale and technology roadmap in memory (including high-bandwidth memory and advanced process execution), plus ongoing capacity investment to support future product generations.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Mar 18, 2026: Micron reported second quarter fiscal 2026 results for the quarter ended Feb 26, 2026 (earnings release and call coverage).
• Jan 26, 2026: Micron broke ground on an advanced wafer fabrication facility in Singapore with an approximately $24B (over 10 years) planned investment and designed end-state of ~700,000 square feet of cleanroom space.
• 2026 (cycle continuation): Coverage also highlighted Micron’s manufacturing build/expansion plans and their relevance to AI memory demand (including collaboration themes tied to next-gen platforms).
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=21.48 with PEG=0.04 suggests an unusually attractive valuation relative to growth in your dataset; this typically indicates either very strong forward growth assumptions or that current earnings are depressed/cyclical.
• Profitability: ROIC=29.16% and Profit Margin=0.4149% suggest solid capital efficiency and profitability (again, confirm the specific margin metric definition because the numeric appears low).
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=1.1387% is positive and larger than several other names on your list; however, your “Next Y” EPS growth is only 0.6691%, implying growth may be back-half loaded or subject to memory-cycle volatility.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 indicates strong factor quality; given memory’s cyclicality, validate whether the model is capturing trough conditions and whether the investment cycle (capacity and product transitions) aligns with your timeframe.

top_stocks.description:
• Centerra Gold Inc. is a gold mining company with operations and development assets, including a focus on producing gold and related by-products.
• Market cap and size classification: mid-cap/large-miner relative to peers; often categorized as a mid-tier precious-metals producer.
• Key competitive advantage: asset base with production capability and project/development roadmap intended to support future output and cost discipline; revenue and profitability are naturally linked to gold price and operating execution.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• Feb 19, 2026: Centerra Gold reported Q4 and full-year 2025 results, delivering production and cost guidance and providing a 2026 outlook as part of its strategy for self-funded growth.
• 2026 (earnings/guidance cycle): The company communicated 2026 guidance alongside quarterly performance updates, emphasizing operational execution and cost guidance.
• 2026 (market context): Commentary around gold-related momentum highlighted how Centerra’s results and outlook can be sensitive to precious-metals price movements and cost levels.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=7.04 and PEG=0.44 indicate comparatively low valuation versus growth; a low P/E in gold miners can also reflect market concerns about the near-term earnings profile or cost volatility, so it’s important to confirm the sustainability of margins.
• Profitability: ROIC=28.20% is strong, and Profit Margin=0.4469% indicates profitability in your metric set; the key is whether margins are currently supported by favorable gold prices and hedging/one-offs or represent durable operating economics.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2129% is modest; with such low growth, the valuation appears to be pricing in limited expansion—so upside likely depends more on gold price, cost reductions, and production reliability than on rapid EPS compounding.
• Quality: Investor Score=95/100 suggests strong overall factor alignment; with the low P/E, the score may be reflecting attractive value + acceptable quality signals, but the primary risks are execution/cost inflation and commodity-cycle dynamics.
